summary: This notebook demonstrates a complete training recipe for a sensor-based gesture recognition competition using a single BERT model trained across five folds. It details a comprehensive preprocessing pipeline that removes gravity from accelerometer data, computes angular velocity and distance from quaternions, and extracts statistical features from Time-of-Flight (ToF) sensor data. The architecture combines modality-specific CNN branches with Squeeze-and-Excitation blocks for IMU, Thm, and ToF inputs, which are then concatenated and processed by a BERT encoder. The author emphasizes proper handling of missing values, sequence padding, and global scaling, ultimately averaging predictions from five EMA-weighted models for inference. Readers should learn how to effectively fuse heterogeneous sensor modalities and leverage pre-trained language models for time-series classification.
competition_flow: Loads precomputed sensor CSV data, applies gravity removal and statistical feature engineering, trains a custom BERT-based multimodal CNN architecture using 5-fold stratified cross-validation, and generates a submission by averaging logits from five EMA-weighted models.
data_reading: Reads training data via pandas.read_csv from a precomputed CSV path.
Uses polars for inference data conversion.
Loads test demographics and test CSV from Kaggle input paths.
data_processing: Removes gravity from accelerometer data using rotation quaternions.
Computes angular velocity and angular distance from quaternions.
Handles missing values with forward/backward fill and custom range-based NaN placeholders.
Pads sequences to the 95th percentile length.
Replaces -1 in ToF data with NaN.
features_engineering: IMU: acceleration magnitude, rotation angle, jerk features, linear acceleration magnitude, angular velocity components, and angular distance.
ToF: raw pixel values plus mean, std, min, max statistics computed over the full 64-pixel vector and split into multiple regional chunks.
models: Custom CMIModel (IMU/Thm/ToF CNN branches + BERT encoder + Linear classifier)
cv_strategy: StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=0) stratified on gesture labels
ensembling: Averages raw logits from five independently trained models (loaded from EMA-weighted checkpoints) across all folds before taking the argmax for final predictions.
insights: Gravity removal is essential for accurate IMU feature extraction.
Statistical aggregations (mean, std, min, max) over ToF sensor regions effectively capture spatial patterns without raw pixel overload.
Padding sequences to the 95th percentile optimizes the trade-off between information retention and computational cost.
BERT successfully models temporal dependencies when fed concatenated, scaled modality features.
critical_findings: Older notebook versions contained a critical bug where only the last fold's model was used for submission, highlighting the importance of averaging all folds.
The value -1 in ToF data represents invalid measurements and must be converted to NaN before processing.
